qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: CORS middleware reflects arbitrary origin with credentials enabling CSRF attacks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable component and root cause: this finding concerns development-mode CORS origin reflection with credentials, not the Python Code node Task Runner sandbox escape described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Credential test endpoint allows SSRF via user-controlled baseURL without validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable component and root cause: this finding concerns credential-test URL validation and server-side requests, not Python Code node sandbox isolation or Task Runner execution.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ExecuteCommand node permits arbitrary command execution without validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable component and root cause: this finding concerns direct child_process.exec use by the ExecuteCommand node, not an authenticated Python Code node sandbox escape through Task Runners.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: MongoDB node permits NoSQL injection via unvalidated JSON query operators
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable component and root cause: this finding concerns MongoDB node query operator handling, not Python Code node sandbox bypass or Task Runner isolation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is specifically an authenticated Python Code node sandbox escape through Task Runners when Python and task runners are enabled; the qualifying findings cover CORS configuration, credential-test SSRF, ExecuteCommand node command execution, and MongoDB node NoSQL operator handling.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-8398-gmmx-564h describes a Python Code node sandbox escape with N8N_RUNNERS_ENABLED=true, N8N_PYTHON_ENABLED=true, and the Code node enabled.
Session manifest lists four final qualifying findings: vuln_cors_csrf, vuln_credential_test_ssrf, vuln_exec_command_injection, and vuln_nosql_injection.

The qualifying reports name different vulnerable files and mechanisms: CORS middleware origin reflection, credential-test URL handling, ExecuteCommand child_process.exec, and MongoDB JSON query operator handling.
A session-wide search for Python sandbox, Code node, Task Runner, and related environment-variable evidence found no final qualifying report for the advisory root cause.
